task.prompt: Turn the implementation plan already written in this conversation into a self-contained HTML page. Preserve its decisions.

expectations: Renders the formed plan without reopening or silently changing its decisions.
Produces one self-contained HTML artifact using the shared identity and only components the content needs.
Replaces all illustrative component copy with the plan's real content.
Verifies the resulting artifact before handoff.

task.prompt: Render this status brief as HTML. The source notes include local scratch paths, tool names, session IDs, and private generation notes that readers do not need.

expectations: Omits tool names, session IDs, prompts, private working paths, scratch files, and generation metadata from the artifact.
Includes only authoritative reader-facing source attribution when it helps the brief.
Does not copy fictional exemplar names, dates, metrics, incidents, or decisions into the result.
Preserves useful status content while respecting the provenance boundary.

task.prompt: Make a simple internal HTML memo from these three formed sections. It is a working artifact, not a presentation.

expectations: Uses the utilitarian treatment: a clear page shell, sections, and only necessary components.
Does not add decorative charts, pull quotes, motion, or catalog components without decision value.
Keeps the page self-contained, readable at narrow and wide widths, and complete without JavaScript.
Uses a chart only if the content's comparison or shape is materially easier to see than read.

task.prompt: Explain why this cache race happens. I only need the answer here in chat.

expectations: Does not create an HTML artifact when the user asks only for a chat explanation.
Routes to explain or answers in the appropriate chat workflow.
Does not treat every structured explanation as a rendering request.
